content: CJD diagnosis: 1) Clinical criteria (rapidly progressive dementia plus ≥2 of: myoclonus, visual/cerebellar signs, pyramidal/extrapyramidal signs, akinetic mutism); 2) MRI brain: cortical ribboning and basal ganglia hyperintensity on DWI/FLAIR (highly sensitive/specific); 3) EEG: periodic sharp wave complexes (specific but insensitive, late finding); 4) CSF: elevated 14-3-3 protein, tau, neuron-specific enolase (markers of rapid neuronal destruction); 5) RT-QuIC (real-time quaking-induced conversion): detects prion seeding activity in CSF, >95% specificity, now CDC case definition criterion; 6) Definitive diagnosis requires brain biopsy or autopsy (spongiform change, neuronal loss, gliosis, PrP deposition). Genetic testing for PRNP mutations in suspected familial cases.
